abstract: Service robots are increasingly adopted in restaurants, yet it remains unclear how firms convert AI investments into operational agility and customer service performance. Drawing on real options theory, hybrid intelligence, and parasocial relationship perspective, this study examines how AI climate, customer–AI assisted exchange (CAIX), and business process innovativeness enhance robot agility, which subsequently improves operational adjustment agility and customer service competencies. Using two independent survey waves from robot-assisted restaurants in Shanghai and Taipei and follow-up practitioner interviews, analyzed with PLS-SEM, the results show that robot agility is the central mechanism translating AI investments into service outcomes. Perceived anthropomorphism strengthens the effect of robot agility on customer service competencies but does not influence operational adjustment agility. The findings demonstrate that effective AI-enabled service depends on aligning organizational readiness, customer intelligence, and adaptive workflows to deliver responsive, personalised, and reliable service.

affiliation: National Kaohsiung University of Hospitality and Tourism
authors_full.name: Edward C.S. Ku

affiliation: National Kaohsiung University of Hospitality and Tourism
authors_full.name: Shu-Hua Wu

ur.ai_reason: AI机器人餐厅中AI气候与感知拟人化影响顾客服务绩效
ur.tldr: 基于真实期权理论、混合智能和拟社会关系，研究通过双波问卷与访谈考察机器人餐厅的AI投资如何转化为机器人敏捷性，进而提升运营调节敏捷和客服能力。结果显示机器人敏捷是核心中介，拟人化感知强化客服效果。

title: Harnessing AI to personalize service delivery: insights from AI-Driven robot restaurants
title_zh: 利用AI实现服务交付个性化：AI驱动机器人餐厅的启示
